spec_match.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP verified CSS-native mode no longer authors text-overflow:clip. Focus and selection keep computed text-overflow:ellipsis while the probe reports the active overflowing interaction state.

interactive.rationale: The page exposes sliders, a content-type select including a short no-overflow edge, CSS-native/JS comparison buttons, focus/select/blur controls, rebuild, and CSS/JS benchmark buttons.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The lede and explanation now frame the demo as Chrome native focus/selection behaviour compared with a JavaScript fallback, not as an authored CSS clip simulation.

edge_cases.rationale: The Short no-overflow edge content type reports overflowed=false, and the narrow-width state reports a resting overflowing ellipsis target.

references.rationale: The labelled references section links to ChromeStatus feature 5146265241387008, the Chrome 148 beta note, and CSS Overflow 3 ellipsis interaction.

design_system.rationale: Button background overrides now include explicit text colours, and the range/select labels are associated with their form controls.

summary: Chrome DevTools MCP on Chrome/151 verified CSS-native focus and selection with computed text-overflow:ellipsis, short no-overflow and narrow overflow states, JS comparison and benchmarks, valid label bindings, and no console or network errors. The feature conformance route reported 5 PASS / 0 FAIL.
